A near-perfect record across diverse, moderate-odds markets is the cross-category signature the long-shot test cannot see. Consistent with informed trading — not proof of it.

scorecard.formula: Poisson-binomial: z = (wins − Σpᵢ) / √Σpᵢ(1−pᵢ) over ALL resolved bets at their own odds
scorecard.numbers: Won 149 of 223 bets across markets the blended odds priced at ~54% — about 120.0 expected by luck (4.6σ above chance, ≈ 1 in 557.6K). A near-perfect record across diverse, moderate-odds markets the long-shot test can't see.

scorecard.formula: distinct surprising events won over time
scorecard.numbers: Won against the odds in 45 separate events — being early-and-right on multiple unrelated surprises is a repeat-offender pattern, not one lucky hit.

scorecard.formula: largest event position ÷ this wallet's median bet
scorecard.numbers: Largest position $24,322 is ~87× this wallet's median bet ($280) — an informed bet that dwarfs its own trading norm (the within-trader size anomaly).

subjects.heroSentence: This account placed a single $10K bet at roughly 12 percent on “maduro-out-in-2025” and cashed out about $82K. One bet is not statistically improbable on its own — but a lone, outsized, high-conviction long-shot like this, alongside the other signals, is the single-bet informed-trading signature. Consistent with informed trading, not proof of it.

permutation.statement: Is this just luck? We took every long-shot bet placed by the 32 flagged wallets — 3,518 bets in all — and used the market's own prices to work out how many they should win by pure guessing: about 1,962. They actually won 2,325. We then re-ran every outcome 10,000 times at random; the group beat chance by 16.3 standard deviations (p < 1e-4) — a margin so large it essentially never happens by accident. Strong evidence the group as a whole was trading on information; not proof for any single wallet.
